question.text: An investor bought mutual fund units at NAV of Rs. 15 and redeemed after 540 days at NAV of Rs. 21. Assuming a non-leap year (365 days), the CAGR is closest to:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CAGR = (End/Begin)^(365/days) - 1 = (21/15)^(365/540) - 1 = (1.4)^(0.6759) - 1 = 1.2672 - 1 = 26.72%. **The Trap:** Simple return = (21-15)/15 = 40% over 540 days. Annualizing simply as 40% × 365/540 = 27.04% is incorrect — CAGR uses geometric compounding. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fractional period CAGR is commonly tested in V-A, V-B, and X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For holding periods under 1 year, CAGR may overstate the annualized return — always mention the actual holding period alongside.
